title: OpenTikZ item metadata
description: Schema for every <name>.meta.json sibling of an icon, template, or example. See docs/DESIGN_GUIDE.md.

id.description: Globally unique, stable identifier (kebab-case). Used by catalog.json and the website.

type.description: Which content layer this item belongs to.

venue.description: Optional: conference/venue widths this item is tuned for (e.g. CVPR, NeurIPS, ACL).

license.description: SPDX identifier. Graphic content is CC0-1.0.

requires.description: LaTeX packages / TikZ libraries the .tex needs in its preamble.

preview.description: Relative path to the generated .svg preview.

description.description: Optional one-line description of the item.

featured.description: Optional: include this item on the marketing Home showcase.

composed_of.description: Optional: ids of the library items this figure is composed of (powers the decomposable showcase / deep-links).

edit_contract.description: Optional (templates only): the machine-readable edit contract the using-opentikz skill consumes. validate.py cross-checks that every parameters[].name and styles[] entry actually appears in the sibling .tex.

parameters.description: The \def macros at the top of the .tex that drive the figure.

type.description: Informal value type, e.g. list<int>, length-cm, text.

default.description: The value shipped in the template.

invariant.description: A constraint the value must satisfy.

node_naming.description: The node-naming scheme skills target, e.g. 'L<layer>-<neuron>, 1-based'.

styles.description: The tikz style names (each appears as <style>/.style= in the .tex).

operations.description: Safe, contract-sanctioned edit operations (prose; not machine-checked).

invariants.description: Rules an edit must never break (prose; not machine-checked).
